faqs.question: How do I know if I have drain flies in my College Point property?
faqs.answer: Drain flies are small, fuzzy-looking flies (1/8 inch long) often seen near sinks, drains, bathrooms, or basements. They have a distinctive moth-like appearance and tend to rest on walls near drains. If you notice these small flies around your College Point home or business, especially near water sources, you likely have a drain fly infestation in your plumbing system.

faqs.question: What does your $350-$550 drain fly treatment in College Point include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive drain fly treatment includes a thorough inspection of all drains and potential breeding sites, professional bio-enzymatic drain treatments to eliminate organic buildup where larvae develop, application of targeted treatments, and follow-up recommendations. The price varies based on property size, severity of infestation, and number of drains requiring treatment in your College Point location.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take to work in College Point homes?
faqs.answer: Most clients notice a significant reduction in drain fly activity within 2-3 days after treatment. Complete elimination typically occurs within 1-2 weeks as the treatment breaks down the organic matter where larvae breed and the existing adult fly population dies off. Our technicians will provide specific timeline expectations based on your College Point property's infestation severity.

faqs.question: Can drain flies return after treatment in College Point?
faqs.answer: While our treatment is highly effective, drain flies can return if conditions allow organic buildup to accumulate again in drains. We provide maintenance recommendations specific to College Point's water conditions and your property type. Regular drain cleaning, proper ventilation, and addressing any plumbing leaks will help prevent re-infestation. We also offer follow-up services and preventative maintenance plans for College Point residents.
